zotero.abstractNote: Deep learning has achieved impressive results in many domains, but often requires large amounts of data and lacks interpretability. In robotics, we have a strong prior: the laws of physics. In this paper, we propose Deep Lagrangian Networks (DeLaN), a deep learning architecture that incorporates the Lagrangian formulation of physics. By learning the Lagrangian of a system, we can derive the equations of motion, which allows us to model the dynamics of a robot. Our approach is data-efficient, interpretable, and can be used for control. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on a simulated robot arm and a real-world robot.
